text: Agile methodology is a project management approach often used in software development. Teams work in short phases called "sprints," usually lasting two to four weeks. Each sprint begins with a plan based on the "backlog," a prioritized list of tasks. These tasks are described as "user stories" — simple explanations of a feature from the end user's perspective. Every day, the team holds a short "standup" meeting to discuss progress and any problems. This structure supports iterative development, meaning the team releases small, working parts of the product regularly. By receiving feedback after each sprint, the team can quickly adapt and improve the final product.
